LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PAUL WARTELLE, FRANCIS PINNOCK AND WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PRONG ONE IS MET BECAUSE THE COMPLAINT IS CENTERED ON DEFENDANT'S PRIOR CLAIM AGAINST PLAINTIFF. PRONG TWO IS MET. IN DORAN V MAGAN (1999) THE COURT HELD THAT THE DENIAL OF A C.C.P. 664.6 MOTION IS SIMPLY THE DENIAL OF THE MOTION AND DOES NOT DISPOSE OF THE ACTIONS. THE PARTIES RETAIN THE ABILITY TO CONTINUE TO LITIGATE THE ISSUES. THUS, A REASONABLE ATTORNEY WOULD BELIEVE THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTION COULD BE CONTINUE. THIS MEANS PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE HIS CLAIM OF MALICIOUS PROSECUTION. IT ALSO WAS NOT AN ABUSE OF PROCESS TO CONTINUE THE ACTION. THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(B), IS A DEFENSE TO BOTH THE ABUSE OF PROCESS AND INVASION OF PRIVACY CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE FEES SOUGHT, $14,000 AND THE COSTS, $1,045, ARE REASONABLE AND THE FEES ARE BASED ON MARKET RATES. WITTE V KAUFMAN (2006) 141 CAL. APP 4TH 1201 IS NOT CONTROLLING. BECAUSE THE FIRM OF WARTELLE, ET AL. IS A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATATION, IT MAY RECOVER FOR THE WORK OF ONE OF ITS EMPLOYEES. P.L.C.M. GROUP, INC. V DREXLER (2000) 22 CAL 4TH 1084. THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS HAD AN ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WITH THE LAW CORPORATION AND ATTORNEYS WEAVER AND MCDONALD. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
